data.body:
REBAR-DEBUG DIAGNOSIS (2026-07-21)

Reproduction/localization: current origin/main src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T8.md says the overlay applies to plans containing "output schemas, enums" without requiring an LLM consumer/producer. The same rubric then asks the reviewer to probe any referenced schema/enum. There is no deterministic applicability guard before the agentic criterion runs.

Root cause: applicability is encoded only as ambiguous prompt prose. Generic typed data vocabulary is therefore a positive LLM-surface signal, so ordinary dataclasses, sentinels, and stdlib enums can engage T8. The existing ANTI-FP sentence requires concrete evidence after engagement but does not prevent false engagement.

Minimal corrective seam: narrow the T8 applicability sentence to schemas/enums consumed by or emitted from an LLM/tool/reviewer contract, add explicit deterministic-code counterexamples, and pin both abstention and a true LLM-schema positive case in criterion eval/unit coverage. Preserve any ordinary data-shape finding under a non-overlay criterion.

No code changed during this diagnostic run.
